concept: Allergy / Immune-Mediated Hypersensitivity to a Stated Agent
description: A suspected or established immune-mediated hypersensitivity to a stated substance or agent under stated clinical criteria. An allergy may have different manifestations and severity across exposures and people. It is not dislike, every intolerance, toxicity, infection, contamination, side effect, adverse effect in general, or proof that a particular exposure caused a current symptom.
sound_symbolism: Disruptive 'whe' marks an unusual response, 'ma' locates it in embodied reaction, and final 'ki' returns the claim to stated criteria. The form carries sensitivity without making the person fragile or impure.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun according to context: 'mia whemaki phelu.' (I have an allergy.) Name the agent, evidence, manifestations, severity, uncertainty, and exact source record where relevant. Distinct from 'pekira' (toxic), 'peshiro' (infection), 'porewi' (side effect), 'whekaro' (adverse effect), and ordinary dislike.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A bounded allergy term supports safety and self-report without extending biological sensitivity into judgments about character or purity.

tags.physical: an immune-mediated bodily response to an agent
